channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Synchronizing Azure Storage Across Isolated Private Endpoint Networks
item.description: Introduction In Azure repatriation programs, enterprises often need to migrate large volumes of blob data from a source Azure environment to a target Azure environment under strict security and network isolation constraints. A typical repatriation setup includes fully isolated source and target...
item.dc:creator: deepakkumarv

item.title: Announcing Public Preview of Argo CD extension on AKS and Azure Arc enabled Kubernetes clusters
item.description: We are excited to announce public preview of the Argo CD extension for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) and Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes clusters. As GitOps becomes the standard for deploying and operating applications at scale, enterprises need a way to implement GitOps while staying compliant with...
item.dc:creator: Poornima99

item.title: New Oracle Autonomous AI Database - Dedicated option on Oracle AI Database@Azure
item.description: At Microsoft, our partnership with Oracle reflects a shared commitment to meeting customers where they are — helping them modernize mission-critical workloads on Azure without sacrificing the performance, control, or compliance posture they've built over years. As part of this continued evolution,...
item.dc:creator: SparshAgrawat

item.title: Hosted Containers and AI Agent Solutions
item.description: If you have built a proof-of-concept AI agent on your laptop and wondered how to turn it into something other people can actually use, you are not alone. The gap between a working prototype and a production-ready service is where most agent projects stall. Hosted containers close that gap faster...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: Take Control of Every Message: Partial Failure Handling for Service Bus Triggers in Azure Functions
item.description: The Problem: All-or-Nothing Batch Processing in Azure Service Bus Azure Service Bus is one of the most widely used messaging services for building event-driven applications on Azure. When you use Azure Functions with a Service Bus trigger in batch mode, your function receives multiple messages at...
item.dc:creator: swapnil_nagar

item.title: SQL code analysis in VS Code: Configure rules without editing your project file
item.description: SQL code analysis has been part of the SSDT workflow for a long time. Before deploying a schema change, you could run a set of static analysis rules against your project to catch potential issues, things like missing primary keys, deprecated syntax, or objects that could break under certain...
item.dc:creator: Iqra Shaikh

item.title: Manage SQL database schemas in VS Code: Publish dialog and item templates
item.description: Making schema changes often means jumping between tools. You write code in VS Code, then switch to a separate tool to deploy your changes : exporting a script, running it manually, or copy-pasting into a query editor. Either way, it pulls you out of your flow. With the latest updates to SQL...
item.dc:creator: Iqra Shaikh

item.title: Agentic Platform Engineering with GitHub Copilot
item.description: We’ve talked about the human scale problem and what happens when infrastructure scales but understanding doesn’t. If you’ve been following along, you know the thesis: our tools have outpaced our ability to operate them, and platform engineering is how we’re fighting back. But here’s the thing –...
item.dc:creator: Diego Casati, Ray Kao

item.title: Get started with NeuBird Hawkeye MCP server in Azure SRE Agent
item.description: Integrate NeuBird Hawkeye MCP with Azure SRE Agent TL;DR If your infrastructure spans multiple clouds say Azure and GCP, or Azure alongside any other cloud provider investigating incidents means jumping between completely separate consoles, log systems, and monitoring stacks. Azure SRE Agent now...
item.dc:creator: dbandaru

item.title: Azure Event Grid MQTT Broker: Enterprise-Grade Messaging for the Connected World
item.description: Azure Messaging · What's New · March 2026 The Enterprise MQTT Brokerfor Every Connected Ecosystem Scale to millions of devices, enforce zero-trust security, and route real-time events across every service, system, and application — all on Azure's hyperscaler-grade messaging backbone. 1,000...
item.dc:creator: Connected-Seth

item.title: Microsoft Sentinel is now supported in Unified RBAC with row-level access
item.description: Enabling streamlined, granular, and scalable permissions We’re excited to announce the Public Preview of Unified Role Based Access Control (URBAC) for Microsoft Sentinel, together with row-level access. This new capability, available in April, extends the Microsoft Defender Unified RBAC model to...
item.dc:creator: tomasbeerthuis

item.title: From code to cloud: Deploy an AI agent to Microsoft Foundry in minutes with azd
item.description: Deploy and monitor an AI agent on Microsoft Foundry using the Azure Developer CLI (azd). This tutorial covers infrastructure scaffolding with Bicep, one-command deployment, local development, and real-time log streaming—all from Visual Studio Code. The post From code to cloud: Deploy an AI agent to...
item.dc:creator: PuiChee (PC) Chan

item.title: Migrating Ant Builds to Maven with GitHub Copilot app modernization
item.description: Many legacy Java applications still rely on Apache Ant for building, packaging, and dependency management. While Ant remains flexible, it lacks the structured lifecycle, dependency resolution, and ecosystem support that modern build tools like Maven provide. Migrating from Ant to Maven improves...
item.dc:creator: ayangupta

item.title: Announcing Aspire 13.2
item.description: Aspire 13.2 brings TypeScript AppHost authoring, an agent-native CLI, Foundry integration, and more. The post Announcing Aspire 13.2 appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: Maddy Montaquila, David Fowler

item.title: Code Optimizations for Azure App Service Now Available in VS Code
item.description: Today we shipped a feature in the Azure App Service extension for VS Code that answers both questions: Code Optimizations, powered by Application Insights profiler data and GitHub Copilot. The problem: production performance is a black box You've deployed your .NET app to Azure App Service....
item.dc:creator: fiveisprime

item.title: Shaping the Next Era of Energy Decision Making with Geospatial Intelligence
item.description: Shaping the Next Era of Energy Decision Making with Geospatial Intelligence By Yves Pitsch, GM, Azure Space, Microsoft The global energy system is entering a new phase—one defined not just by a single transition to renewables, but by the convergence of forces that are reshaping how energy is...
item.dc:creator: Yves-Pitsch

item.title: How to get blob Total Blob Count and Total Capacity with Blob Inventory
item.description: Table of Contents Approach Introduction to the Blob Inventory Service Steps to enable inventory report Support Documentation Disclaimer: Approach This article presents how to take advantage of the Blob Inventory service to get the total blob count and the total capacity per storage account, per...
item.dc:creator: ruineiva

item.title: Reliable blob processing using Azure Logic Apps: Recommended architecture
item.description: Understanding the Limitations of In-App Blob Triggers The Logic App Blob - In-App trigger https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/logic-apps/connectors/built-in/reference/azureblob/ is built upon the same architecture as the Azure Function App Blob trigger and therefore inherits similar features...
item.dc:creator: SwathiK

item.title: Building real-world AI automation with Foundry Local and the Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description: A hands-on guide to building real-world AI automation with Foundry Local, the Microsoft Agent Framework, and PyBullet. No cloud subscription, no API keys, no internet required. Why Developers Should Care About Offline AI Imagine telling a robot arm to "pick up the cube" and watching it execute the...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: Securing Azure AI Agents: Identity, Access Control, and Guardrails in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: As AI agents evolve from simple chatbots to autonomous systems that access enterprise data, call APIs, and orchestrate workflows, security becomes non negotiable. Unlike traditional applications, AI agents introduce new risks — such as prompt injection, over privileged access, unsafe tool...
item.dc:creator: SudhaS

item.title: Announcing public preview: Cilium mTLS encryption for Azure Kubernetes Service
item.description: We are thrilled to announce the public preview of Cilium mTLS encryption in Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS), delivered as part of Advanced Container Networking Services and powered by the Azure CNI dataplane built on Cilium. This capability is the result of a close engineering collaboration between...
item.dc:creator: chandanAggarwal

item.title: Run OpenClaw Agents on Azure Linux VMs (with Secure Defaults)
item.description: Many teams want an enterprise-ready personal AI assistant, but they need it on infrastructure they control, with security boundaries they can explain to IT. That is exactly where OpenClaw fits on Azure. OpenClaw is a self-hosted, always-on personal agent runtime you run in your enterprise...
item.dc:creator: johnsonshi_msft

item.title: What’s new in Microsoft Sentinel: RSAC 2026
item.description: Security is entering a new era, one defined by explosive data growth, increasingly sophisticated threats, and the rise of AI-enabled operations. To keep pace, security teams need an AI-powered approach to collect, reason over, and act on security data at scale. At RSA Conference 2026 (RSAC), we’re...
item.dc:creator: spalani

item.title: Detect, correlate, contain: New Azure Firewall IDPS detections in Microsoft Sentinel and XDR
item.description: As threat actors continue to blend reconnaissance, exploitation, and post-compromise activity, network-level signals remain critical for early detection and correlated response. To strengthen this layer, we're introducing five new Azure Firewall IDPS detections, now available out of the box in the...
item.dc:creator: Mohit_Kumar

item.title: Agentic Use Cases for Developers on the Microsoft Sentinel Platform
item.description: Interested in building an agent with Sentinel platform solutions but not sure where to start? This blog will help you understand some common use cases for agent development that we’ve seen across our partner ecosystem. SOC teams don’t need more alerts - they need fast, repeatable investigation and...
item.dc:creator: Sai_Marapareddy

item.title: Extending App Assure’s Sentinel Promise through the Sentinel Advisory Service
item.description: At RSAC last year, we introduced the Microsoft Sentinel Promise with a straightforward commitment to our customers: that third-party data ingestion for Sentinel is reliable, predictable, and scalable without the need for complex custom coding and architecting. In other words, your connectors for...
item.dc:creator: MikeAdams

item.title: RSAC 2026: New Microsoft Sentinel Connectors Announcement
item.description: Microsoft Sentinel helps organizations detect, investigate, and respond to security threats across increasingly complex environments. With the rollout of the Microsoft Sentinel data lake in the fall, and the App Assure-backed Sentinel promise that supports it, customers now have access to...
item.dc:creator: JesseKopavi

item.title: How Granular Delegated Admin Privileges (GDAP) allows Sentinel customers to delegate access
item.description: Simplifying Defender SIEM and XDR delegated access As Microsoft Sentinel and Defender converge into a unified experience, organizations face a fundamental challenge: the lack of a scalable, comprehensive, delegated access model that works seamlessly across Entra ID and Sentinel’s Azure Resource...
item.dc:creator: Yossi Basha

item.title: Defending the AI Era: New Microsoft Capabilities to Protect AI
item.description: As enterprises rapidly adopt AI to drive productivity, automate decisions, and power intelligent agents, a new attack surface is emerging—one that traditional security controls were never designed to protect. AI models, training pipelines, plugins, and autonomous agents now sit directly in the path...
item.dc:creator: danielacardon

item.title: New innovations in Microsoft Defender to strengthen multi-cloud, containers, and AI model security
item.description: Cloud security today is no longer just about misconfigurations; it’s about keeping pace with cloud-native change, prioritizing risk before it becomes an incident, and securing AI as a new supply chain for applications. In modern environments, infrastructure and applications are rebuilt and...
item.dc:creator: parulseth

item.title: Heroku Entered Maintenance Mode — Here's Your Next Move
item.description: Heroku has entered sustaining engineering — no new features, no new enterprise contracts. If you're running production workloads on the platform, you're probably thinking about what comes next. Azure Container Apps is worth a serious look. Scale-to-zero pricing, event-driven autoscaling, built-in...
item.dc:creator: simonjj

item.title: Designing an Azure Disaster Recovery Strategy for Enterprise Workloads
item.description: Introduction As organizations accelerate their cloud adoption journey, ensuring resiliency and disaster recovery (DR) becomes a foundational requirement for maintaining business continuity. In large-scale Azure environments, customers often operate critical workloads in a primary region and later...
item.dc:creator: Shikhaghildiyal

item.title: Power Apps Vibe Experience: Build Business Apps at the Speed of Ideas
item.description: Power Apps Vibe Experience: Building Business Applications with AI in Minutes Organizations today operate in a fast-paced digital environment where new business challenges emerge constantly. Whether it’s managing internal workflows, tracking projects, or collecting customer feedback, businesses...
item.dc:creator: harshul05

item.title: Centralized Monitoring in Azure
item.description: Centralized Monitoring in Azure: Automating Diagnostic Settings Across All Resources Introduction In modern cloud environments, observability is not optional—it’s foundational. As organizations scale their Azure footprint, ensuring consistent monitoring across hundreds (or thousands) of resources...
item.dc:creator: Shikhaghildiyal

item.title: Introducing Azure Managed Grafana MCP: The Managed Telemetry Gateway for AI Agents
item.description: AI agents are rapidly becoming a core part of how teams build, operate, and improve cloud systems, from coding assistants to autonomous remediation workflows. To deliver on that promise in the enterprise, agents need a secure, governed way to access real production telemetry. Azure Managed Grafana...
item.dc:creator: aayodeji

item.title: Building an Enterprise Platform for Inference at Scale
item.description: Architecture Decisions With the optimization stack in place, the next layer of decisions is architectural — how you distribute compute across GPUs, nodes, and deployment environments to match your model size and traffic profile. GPU Parallelism Strategy on AKS Strategy How It Works When to Use...
item.dc:creator: bobmital

item.title: Remote MCP Server preview in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Earlier this week we release the public preview for our Azure DevOps MCP Server. Today we are excited to let you know that the Azure DevOps MCP Server is now available to use in Microsoft Foundry. For those who are new to Foundry, Microsoft Foundry is a unified platform for building and managing AI...
item.dc:creator: Dan Hellem

item.title: Azure Front Door: Resiliency Series – Part 2: Faster recovery (RTO)
item.description: In Part 1 of this blog series, we outlined our four‑pillar strategy for resiliency in Azure Front Door: configuration resiliency, data plane resiliency, tenant isolation, and accelerated Recovery Time Objective (RTO). Together, these pillars help Azure Front Door remain continuously available and...
item.dc:creator: AbhishekTiwari

item.title: Azure Bastion: Enterprise-grade secure access made simple
item.description: Managing secure remote access to virtual machines traditionally means juggling public IP addresses, configuring jump boxes, deploying VPN infrastructure, and managing complex firewall rules. Each layer adds cost, complexity, and potential security vulnerabilities. Azure Bastion changes everything....
item.dc:creator: ShabazShaik

item.title: Building Knowledge-Grounded AI Agents with Foundry IQ
item.description: Foundry IQ now integrates with Foundry Agent Service via MCP (Model Context Protocol), enabling developers to build AI agents grounded in enterprise knowledge. This integration combines Foundry IQ’s intelligent retrieval capabilities with Foundry Agent Service’s orchestration, enabling agents to...
item.dc:creator: NelsonKumari

item.title: From Azure IoT Operations Data Processor Pipelines to Dataflows
item.description: In this post we explore the evolution from Azure IoT Operations Data Processor Pipelines to Dataflows, why we adopted a hybrid strategy with custom Rust pods, and the architectural lessons we learned building event detection systems at the edge. The post From Azure IoT Operations Data Processor...
item.dc:creator: Maho Pacheco

item.title: Azure NCv6 Virtual Machines: Enhancements and GA Transition
item.description: NCv6 Virtual Machines are Azure's flexible, next generation platform enabling both leading-edge graphics and generative AI compute workloads. Featuring NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition (BSE) GPUs, Intel Xeon™ 6 "Granite Rapids" 6900P series CPUs, and a suite of Microsoft Azure...
item.dc:creator: Fernando_Aznar

item.title: Authentication Tokens Are Not a Data Contract
item.description: Authentication tokens exist to answer one question: is this caller authorized to do this? They are not intended to be a stable data interface, a schema you can depend on, or an input into application logic. If your application decodes tokens and reads claims from them, this is an important...
item.dc:creator: Angel Wong

item.title: Announcing the New Home for the Azure Databricks Blog
item.description: We’re excited to share that the Azure Databricks blog has moved to a new address on Microsoft Tech Community Hub! Azure Databricks | Microsoft Community Hub Our new blog home is designed to make it easier than ever for you to discover the latest product updates, deep technical insights, and...
item.dc:creator: AnaviNahar

item.title: The Swarm Diaries: What Happens When You Let AI Agents Loose on a Codebase
item.description: The Idea Single-agent coding assistants are impressive, but they have a fundamental bottleneck: they think serially. Ask one to build a full CLI app with a database layer, a command parser, pretty output, and tests, and it’ll grind through each piece one by one. Industry benchmarks bear this out:...
item.dc:creator: larohra

item.title: Azure Developer CLI (azd): Run and test AI agents locally with azd
item.description: New azd ai agent run and invoke commands let you start and test AI agents from your terminal—locally or in the cloud. The post Azure Developer CLI (azd): Run and test AI agents locally with azd appeared first on Azure SDK Blog.
item.dc:creator: PuiChee (PC) Chan, Travis Angevine

item.title: DiskANN Vector Index Improvements
item.description: Remember when we announced the Public Preview of DiskANN vector indexes back in November and mentioned that once you created the index, your table became read‑only? Yeah… about that… 😅 We shipped early because the demand for Vector search in SQL was overwhelming. We knew the constraints weren’t...
item.dc:creator: Davide Mauri, Pooja Kamath

item.title: Microsoft partners with DataBahn to accelerate enterprise deployments for Microsoft Sentinel
item.description: Enterprise security teams are collecting more telemetry than ever across cloud platforms, endpoints, SaaS applications, and on-premises infrastructure. Security teams want broader data coverage and longer retention without losing control of cost and data quality. This post explains the new DataBahn...
item.dc:creator: JamesAde

item.title: When Infrastructure Scales But Understanding Doesn’t
item.description: We all know this, even if we don’t like to admit it: modern infrastructure can scale infinitely, but human understanding doesn’t. We’ve all seen it happen – organizations going from managing dozens of servers to thousands of containers, from deploying weekly to deploying hundreds of times per day,...
item.dc:creator: Diego Casati, Ray Kao

item.title: Introducing Lakeflow Connect Free Tier, now available on Azure Databricks
item.description: We're excited to introduce the Lakeflow Connect Free Tier on Azure Databricks, so you can easily bring your enterprise data into your lakehouse to build analytics and AI applications faster. Modern applications require reliable access to operational data, especially for training analytics and AI...
item.dc:creator: AnaviNahar
